title: lupinus alpestris var. argentatus
intro: Lupinus alpestris var. argentatus, also known as Silvery Lupine or Argent Lupine, is a plant species belonging to the lupine family. It is a perennial herb with blue-purple flowers, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant's roots contain a variety of alkaloids, which are used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. Lupinus alpestris var. argentatus is used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions, and is also used to promote wound healing and reduce fever.
